title: mitrasacme pygmaea var. confertifolia
intro: Mitrasacme pygmaea var. confertifolia, also known as Dwarf Mitrasacme, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and dark green, needle-like leaves. It is used in traditional medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's extracts have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting against microbial infections. In herbal preparations, Dwarf Mitrasacme is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
